refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR combined the entire load→travel→dump cycle into a single requirement, which includes multiple distinct phases. To be atomic and solution-neutral, the FR is split into sub-FRs each addressing one phase of the cycle. This refinement focuses on the load phase as one atomic function.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the cycle time for the load phase under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, verifying that the 95th percentile cycle time (p95) is less than or equal to the target specified by the customer.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the travel phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.
The NGHE solution shall enable achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the dump phase of the load→travel→dump cycle.

candidate_dps: Optimize load mechanism speed and efficiency
Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback
Design load phase process to minimize idle and transition times
Allocate sufficient power and torque resources to meet cycle time targets
Integrate load phase operations with upstream and downstream processes for smooth flow
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback

potential_sources: Integration complexity with other cycle phases
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on adaptive control which logically isolates the load phase timing optimization, reducing coupling with other system functions. Potential coupling arises from reliance on accurate real-time feedback and coordination with other phases, but these can be managed with clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Real-time feedback data is reliable and timely
Control algorithms can respond quickly enough to influence cycle time
Operational policies allow adaptive control strategies
Site conditions remain within expected parameters
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces and data exchange protocols between load phase control and other cycle phases to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for overall system control and adaptive cycle time management

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time feedback data for adaptive control

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated cycle time commands to execution modules

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as adaptive cycle time control is a core control function; minimal diffusion as it mainly interacts via telemetry and tasking interfaces.
Partial containment possible but would require spreading control logic into vehicle systems, increasing diffusion.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control, not cycle time adaptation; would cause significant diffusion.
Safety services are orthogonal to adaptive cycle time control; containment is weak and diffusion high.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for implementing adaptive cycle time control because it centralizes control logic and manages real-time feedback integration, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may be affected by network latency or failures.
Risk of coupling with tasking interface leading to tight integration that could complicate module updates.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement adaptive cycle time control based on real-time feedback' directly addresses the FR of achieving the customer-defined cycle time target for the load phase by adapting cycle time dynamically, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of meeting p95 ≤ CT_target under operational conditions.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data which may be affected by network latency or failures.
Risk of coupling with tasking interface leading to tight integration that could complicate module updates.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion, as it centralizes the adaptive cycle time control logic and interfaces primarily through telemetry and tasking. Other modules have lower containment scores and higher diffusion, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the implementation boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the cycle time p95 against the CT_target under the agreed duty cycle and site conditions, as specified in the acceptance criteria. The telemetry interface provides real-time feedback necessary for adaptive control verification.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the adaptive cycle time control function to the NGHE Solution module is clear and well-justified, with explicit interfaces defined for telemetry input and tasking output, supporting traceability from FR through DP to IM.

recommended_revisions: Consider implementing fallback or buffering strategies to mitigate risks from telemetry data latency or failures to ensure robust adaptive control.
Define clear interface contracts and modular boundaries for the tasking interface to minimize tight coupling and facilitate independent module updates.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition adequately addresses the functional requirement with a well-defined design parameter and implementation module. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion, making further decomposition unnecessary at this stage. Although there are some diffusion risks related to telemetry data latency and tasking interface coupling, these are manageable through recommended revisions rather than requiring further functional decomposition.

revise.why: To mitigate identified risks, implement fallback or buffering strategies for telemetry data latency/failures and define clear interface contracts to minimize tight coupling in the tasking interface.

assumptions: Telemetry data will be sufficiently reliable with fallback mechanisms.
The NGHE Solution module can encapsulate adaptive control logic effectively.
open_risks: Potential dependency on timely and accurate telemetry data affected by network latency or failures.
Risk of tight coupling with tasking interface complicating module updates.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing cycle time p95 meets CT_target under operational conditions.
Validation of fallback strategies effectiveness for telemetry data issues.
Demonstration of modular boundaries and interface contracts reducing coupling risks.
